text.title: Inference Cost Unit Economics Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed when applying inference-cost-unit-economics. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix keyed to the methodology.

antipattern.symptom: Agent emits a report that omits required fields the schema enforces (skips citation, leaves rationale blank, drops postconditions).
antipattern.root-cause: Author treats the contract as advisory rather than enforceable; no validator wired into the publish step.
antipattern.fix: Wire `scripts/validate-inference-cost-unit-economics.py` into the publish step (CI / pre-commit / orchestrator gate). Reject the artefact until the schema passes.

antipattern.symptom: Agent applies the methodology end-to-end on inputs that should have routed to a different methodology (Applies-If unmet, Skip-If hit).
antipattern.root-cause: 06-decision-tree never walked because the agent jumped straight to the procedure / contract.
antipattern.fix: Make the decision tree a hard gate at step 1 of the procedure. The validator cross-checks `tree_walked == true` before accepting the artefact.

antipattern.symptom: Artefact cites sources / numbers / dependencies that were correct months ago but no longer apply.
antipattern.root-cause: Refresh cadence not part of the methodology; nobody owns 'last_reviewed'.
antipattern.fix: Cap artefact freshness at 30 days for fast-moving topics, 90 days for slow-moving. Add a revisit-trigger field to the schema. The validator rejects artefacts older than the cap.

antipattern.symptom: Output reads like it follows 01-core-rules.xml but the verdicts on individual rules are not recorded — reviewer cannot verify each rule was checked.
antipattern.root-cause: Agent paraphrased the rules instead of consuming them. No per-rule evidence pointer.
antipattern.fix: Force the artefact to carry a per-rule verdict + evidence_pointer field (already enforced by the schema where applicable). Rejects bypass paraphrase.
